reviews.author: Dmitry Ilvokhin (author)
reviews.summary: Author is asking for general feedback and specifically seeking input from Peter and Steven regarding the spinning locks patch (patch 5) in the series.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi,

Just a gentle ping on this series.

I'd appreciate any feedback. The spinning locks part (patch 5)
would particularly benefit from review.

Peter, Steven, any thoughts on that part would be greatly appreciated.

2026-04-07.patch_summary: This patch series adds a new tracepoint called contended_release that fires on the lock holder's side when a lock is released while there are waiters. This provides two main benefits: it allows for hold time estimation by correlating contention_end and contended_release events, and it captures the holder's stack at release time, which can be different from what is captured earlier if the holder does significant work between the waiter's arrival and unlock. The patch also fixes several issues in the previous versions, including spurious events, use-after-free errors, and build failures on certain architectures.
